editor_prompt_text:
You are the Whetstone editor.
Apply or decline feedback according to the spec authority model.

Return only a single JSON object matching editor_summary.json.
Do not return markdown, explanation, fenced code, an editor object, or a decisions array.
The top-level object MUST set round_number to 2.
The draft_before_hash MUST be 0d51e4de4caebe403d883bd09389d62dfe8cca5044d2e69cad9e8b0e57a41041.
This live round may mutate the draft after validation.
If you change the draft, include draft_after_content as the complete revised draft text.
If you make no draft changes, set draft_after_content to the original draft text.
The top-level object MUST include exactly these fields:
- round_number
- draft_before_hash
- draft_after_hash
- accepted_feedback_ids
- modified_feedback_ids
- declined_feedback
- created_conflict_ids
- resolved_issue_ids
- unresolved_issue_ids
- draft_after_content

Use accepted_feedback_ids for feedback accepted as written.
Use modified_feedback_ids for feedback accepted in substance but implemented differently.
Use declined_feedback only for feedback declined under the allowed decline taxonomy.
Use resolved_issue_ids only when the current draft_after resolves the issue.
Use unresolved_issue_ids for issues still unresolved in the current draft_after.
If there are no entries for an array field, return an empty array for that field.

Reviewer feedback JSON:
{
  "draft_hash": "0d51e4de4caebe403d883bd09389d62dfe8cca5044d2e69cad9e8b0e57a41041",
  "feedback": [
    {
      "affected_sections": [
        "Failure Handling",
        "Subsequent Cycles"
      ],
      "authority_impact": "major",
      "baseline_severity": "blocker",
      "claim": "The spec does not define whether sha256_previous is updated when a notification send fails, so conforming implementations will diverge on all subsequent poll cycles after any failure.",
      "determinism_impact": "major",
      "evidence": "Subsequent Cycles step 3 states 'MUST send a notification \u2026 and then set sha256_previous = sha256_current', which implies the update follows a successful send. The Failure Handling section instructs the system to log and continue polling but is entirely silent on sha256_previous. If sha256_previous is not updated the system will reattempt notification on the next cycle whenever the hash still differs; if it is updated no retry ever occurs. Both readings are consistent with the written text.",
      "feedback_id": "fb_001",
      "in_scope": true,
      "invariant_violated": "State transitions must be fully specified for all execution paths so that independent implementations converge on identical behavior.",
      "issue_fingerprint": "a7f3c2d1e5b9084620fd3a1c7e9b52f6d0e3a1b7c9f528d4067e3c1a5b9f20d4",
      "issue_id": "iss_3c9ab1f7e20d5864",
      "issue_type": "under_specified",
      "normalized_severity": "blocker",
      "oscillation_key": null,
      "recommended_change": "Add an explicit sentence to Failure Handling, for example: 'On send failure, sha256_previous MUST NOT be updated; the system will reattempt notification on the next poll cycle if the hash still differs.' Alternatively, if retry is undesired: 'On send failure, sha256_previous MUST be set to sha256_current so that no duplicate notification is attempted.'",
      "rubric_impact": null,
      "severity_rationale": "Two strictly conforming implementations will produce different observable outputs\u2014repeated vs. one-shot notifications\u2014after a single send failure. This is a hard determinism blocker."
    },
    {
      "affected_sections": [
        "Notification Format",
        "Failure Handling"
      ],
      "authority_impact": "nit",
      "baseline_severity": "minor",
      "claim": "RFC3339 permits multiple valid string representations of the same UTC instant, so two implementations can produce different timestamp strings in the notification body and failure log for the same moment.",
      "determinism_impact": "minor",
      "evidence": "Notification Format specifies 'timestamp_utc: {RFC3339 timestamp}'; Failure Handling specifies 'timestamp_utc (RFC3339)' in the JSONL record. RFC3339 section 4.1 permits both 'Z' and '+00:00' for UTC offset, and section 5.6 allows optional fractional seconds with any number of digits. For the same instant, '2026-05-02T12:00:00Z', '2026-05-02T12:00:00.000Z', and '2026-05-02T12:00:00+00:00' are all valid RFC3339 strings.",
      "feedback_id": "fb_002",
      "in_scope": true,
      "invariant_violated": "Output formats must be narrowed to a single canonical representation so that byte-identical output is achievable across implementations.",
      "issue_fingerprint": "b8e4d2a0f6c3951748b2e0d4a8f1c7b3952e6d0a4f8b2e1c9d7a5f3b0e8c4a2f",
      "issue_id": "iss_7b2e4f9c1a3d5e8f",
      "issue_type": "ambiguous_format",
      "normalized_severity": "minor",
      "oscillation_key": null,
      "recommended_change": "Narrow to a canonical sub-profile, for example: 'RFC3339 UTC using the Z suffix with no fractional seconds, e.g., 2026-05-02T12:00:00Z.'",
      "rubric_impact": null,
      "severity_rationale": "Semantic meaning is preserved across all valid representations; this is a string-level rather than behavioral divergence, warranting minor rather than blocker."
    },
    {
      "affected_sections": [
        "Behavior"
      ],
      "authority_impact": "nit",
      "baseline_severity": "nit",
      "claim": "'Polls watch_path every poll_interval_seconds' does not state whether the interval is measured from poll start to next poll start or from poll end to next poll start.",
      "determinism_impact": "nit",
      "evidence": "The Behavior section states only 'The system polls watch_path every poll_interval_seconds.' Two common scheduling models\u2014fixed-period (start-to-start) and sleep-after-completion (end-to-start)\u2014are both consistent with this phrasing and will produce different poll timestamps whenever a poll cycle takes non-negligible time.",
      "feedback_id": "fb_003",
      "in_scope": true,
      "invariant_violated": "Timing semantics must be unambiguous so that scheduling behavior is reproducible across implementations.",
      "issue_fingerprint": "c1d3e5f7a9b1c3d5e7f9a1b3c5d7e9f1a3b5c7d9e1f3a5b7c9d1e3f5a7b9c1d3",
      "issue_id": "iss_5e1c7f3a9b2d4e6f",
      "issue_type": "under_specified",
      "normalized_severity": "nit",
      "oscillation_key": null,
      "recommended_change": "Specify the reference point, for example: 'The system schedules each poll to begin poll_interval_seconds after the start of the previous poll, independently of how long the poll itself takes.'",
      "rubric_impact": null,
      "severity_rationale": "For typical poll durations much shorter than the interval the observable difference is negligible; this is a determinism nit rather than a functional gap."
    }
  ],
  "profile": "determinism",
  "reviewer": {
    "model": "claude-sonnet-4-6",
    "name": "Whetstone Determinism Reviewer",
    "version": "1.0.0"
  },
  "round_number": 2
}

Draft:
# Toy Notification Spec

## Purpose

Send a notification when a watched file changes.

## Roles

- Watcher: observes file changes.
- Sender: sends notifications.
- Operator: configures the watched file and destination.

## Behavior

The system polls `watch_path` every `poll_interval_seconds`.

### Change Definition

A file is considered **changed** when the SHA-256 hash of its byte content differs from the hash recorded for the previous poll cycle.

### First-Run Baseline

On the first poll cycle after startup, the system records the file's current SHA-256 hash as the baseline and MUST NOT send a notification.

### Subsequent Cycles

On each subsequent poll cycle:

1. Read the file bytes from `watch_path`.
2. Compute `sha256_current` over those bytes.
3. If `sha256_current != sha256_previous`, the system MUST send a notification (see **Notification Format**) and then set `sha256_previous = sha256_current`.
4. If `sha256_current == sha256_previous`, no notification is sent.

## Notification Format

- Transport: an email message addressed to `destination`, which MUST be an email address.
- Subject: `ToyNotify: file changed: {watch_path}`
- Body (plain text):
  - `watch_path: {watch_path}`
  - `timestamp_utc: {RFC3339 timestamp}`
  - `sha256: {sha256_current}`

## Configuration

```yaml
watch_path: ./input.txt
destination: ops@example.com
poll_interval_seconds: 60
failure_log_path: ./toy_notify_failures.log
```

## Failure Handling

If sending fails, the system MUST append a single-line JSON object to `failure_log_path` (JSONL format) with keys:

- `timestamp_utc` (RFC3339)
- `watch_path`
- `destination`
- `error`

After recording the failure, the system MUST continue polling on the normal schedule.
prompt_text:
You are the Whetstone reviewer.
Review profile: determinism
Phase: phase_1

Return only a single JSON object. Do not return markdown, explanation, or fenced code.
The top-level object MUST match reviewer_feedback.json and include:
- round_number: 2
- profile: determinism
- reviewer: object with concrete name, version, and model strings
- draft_hash: 0d51e4de4caebe403d883bd09389d62dfe8cca5044d2e69cad9e8b0e57a41041
- feedback: array of feedback items, or [] if no issues are found

Each feedback item MUST represent exactly one issue and include all required schema fields.
The reviewer MUST author issue_id and issue_fingerprint as stable identifiers for the item.
issue_id MUST match iss_[16 lowercase hex characters], for example iss_0123456789abcdef.
issue_fingerprint MUST be exactly 64 lowercase hex characters.
draft_hash MUST exactly equal the draft_hash value supplied above.
Severity fields MUST use only blocker, major, minor, nit, or null. Do not use high, medium, low, none, n/a, or similar aliases.
Each feedback item MUST include exactly these base fields:
- feedback_id
- issue_id
- issue_fingerprint
- issue_type
- affected_sections
- baseline_severity
- authority_impact
- determinism_impact
- rubric_impact
- normalized_severity
- invariant_violated
- claim
- evidence
- recommended_change
- in_scope
- severity_rationale
- oscillation_key
For Phase 1, set oscillation_key to null.

Rubric:
(no rubric provided)
